books.title: Great Expectations
books.author: Charles Dickens
books.summary: An orphan boy living in Victorian England begins his life in extreme poverty and goes on to experience good fortune, love, rejection, wealth, and social challenges as he grows into adulthood.

books.title: The Black Arrow
books.author: Robert Louis Stevenson
books.summary: During the War of the Roses, a young knight must rescue his lady and find out who killed his father.

books.title: The Story of My Experiments with Truth
books.author: Mahatma Gandhi
books.summary: Gandhi relates his life experiences from his birth in Gujarat in 1869 through the Indian National Congress of 1915.

books.title: Queen Victoria
books.author: Lytton Strachey
books.summary: A sardonic and generally unflattering biography of Victoria Alexandrina, Queen of England.

books.title: The Story of the Treasure Seekers
books.author: E. Nesbit
books.summary: Six siblings search for treasure in order to restore their lost family fortune.

books.title: The Four Men
books.author: Hilaire Belloc
books.summary: Hilaire Belloc takes a pilgrimage across his beloved home English county of Sussex writing as four distinct personalities, with a wide variety of songs, stories, and digressions.

books.title: Henry VI, Part II
books.author: William Shakespeare
books.summary: King Henry VI marries Margaret of Anjou and further stokes discord within his court.

books.title: Vathek
books.author: William Beckford
books.summary: A powerful caliph embarks on a quest to harness the powers of darkness.

books.title: Dick Sands, the Boy Captain
books.author: Jules Verne
books.summary: A young sea apprentice finds himself in charge of a ship, which is led astray to a land where the slave trade abounds.

books.title: The Wolf-Leader
books.author: Alexandre Dumas
books.summary: A shoemaker makes a compact with a black werewolf to exchange strands of his hair for granted wishes.

books.title: My Disillusionment in Russia
books.author: Emma Goldman
books.summary: Emma Goldman recounts her experiences living in the early Soviet Union.

books.title: Inspector French’s Greatest Case
books.author: Freeman Wills Crofts
books.summary: An inspector is called in to investigate the murder of a diamond trading firm’s employee and the theft of their jewels.

books.title: The Practice and Theory of Bolshevism
books.author: Bertrand Russell
books.summary: Bertrand Russell travels to post-revolutionary Russia to see if Bolshevism achieves its communist aspirations.

books.title: The Servile State
books.author: Hilaire Belloc
books.summary: Hilaire Belloc’s thesis that the capitalist state breeds a collectivist theory which, in action, produces something utterly different from collectivism: the Servile State.

books.title: A Cycle of the West
books.author: John G. Neihardt
books.summary: A series of epic poems that recounts the history, legends, and mythology of the American West.

books.title: A Woman of No Importance
books.author: Oscar Wilde
books.summary: A mother’s secretive past clashes with high society when her son is offered an irresistible position with a charismatic nobleman.

books.title: Planet of the Damned
books.author: Harry Harrison
books.summary: A planetary champion finds himself employed to solve another planet’s pressing problem.

books.title: Short Fiction
books.author: Gustave Flaubert
books.summary: A collection of short stories by Gustave Flaubert.

books.title: The Necromancers
books.author: Robert Hugh Benson
books.summary: A young woman watches with concern as her adopted brother turns to irreligious forces in the hopes of reconnecting with his dead fiancée.

books.title: Roughing It
books.author: Mark Twain
books.summary: Mark Twain’s semiautobiographical account of his travel across the United States.
